contributingFactors: data-writer sidecar uses STORAGE EXTERNAL on the payload column, disabling PostgreSQL TOAST compression and maximizing disk footprint per row
Each batch inserts 500 rows with large string_agg(md5(random())) payloads (128 repetitions × 500 series), resulting in very large per-row data
No data retention policy, table truncation, or cleanup mechanism exists — data accumulates indefinitely
PVC provisioned at only 512Mi (filesystem-available ~448 MiB) which is undersized for a write-heavy workload of this intensity
predict_linear(available_bytes[30m], 3600) returns negative value (-332 MiB), confirming exhaustion within 1 hour
rootCauseAnalysis.summary: PVC data-service-data in demo-archive is being exhausted by a data-writer sidecar container that continuously inserts 500 large uncompressed rows every 30 seconds into PostgreSQL with no data retention or cleanup, consuming ~400 MiB/hour against a 512Mi volume. The predict_linear function confirms exhaustion within 1 hour.

analysis.rootCauseSummary: PVC data-service-data in demo-archive is being exhausted by a data-writer sidecar container that continuously inserts 500 large uncompressed rows every 30 seconds into PostgreSQL with no data retention or cleanup, consuming ~400 MiB/hour against a 512Mi volume. The predict_linear function confirms exhaustion within 1 hour.

selectedWorkflow.rationale: The expand-pvc-v1 workflow directly addresses the immediate threat of PVC exhaustion within 1 hour. The PVC is backed by StorageClass lvms-vg1 (CSI-based LVMS), which supports online volume expansion. Expanding the PVC buys critical time to prevent a PostgreSQL crash and data loss while the underlying write-rate and retention issues are addressed separately. This is the correct emergency mitigation — GracefulRestart was explicitly ruled out because the root cause (unbounded data accumulation) would immediately recur after any restart.

kaResponse.analysis: PVC data-service-data in demo-archive is being exhausted by a data-writer sidecar container that continuously inserts 500 large uncompressed rows every 30 seconds into PostgreSQL with no data retention or cleanup, consuming ~400 MiB/hour against a 512Mi volume. The predict_linear function confirms exhaustion within 1 hour.

rootCauseAnalysis.summary: PVC data-service-data in demo-archive is being exhausted by a data-writer sidecar container that continuously inserts 500 large uncompressed rows every 30 seconds into PostgreSQL with no data retention or cleanup, consuming ~400 MiB/hour against a 512Mi volume. The predict_linear function confirms exhaustion within 1 hour.
causalChain: Signal: PVRunwayShort — PVC data-service-data in demo-archive projected to exhaust capacity within 1 hour
Why? PVC used bytes growing at ~400 MiB/hour against a 448 MiB filesystem capacity (512Mi provisioned)
Why? The data-writer sidecar container continuously inserts 500 large-payload rows every 30 seconds into PostgreSQL with no cleanup or retention
Why? The audit_log table payload column uses STORAGE EXTERNAL, disabling TOAST compression and maximizing disk usage per row; each row contains a multi-kilobyte uncompressed MD5 string

Root cause: The data-service Deployment's data-writer container is misconfigured — it generates unbounded write load with uncompressed large payloads into a 512Mi PVC that has no data retention mechanism and is undersized for this workload

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Targeting Deployment/data-service because it contains the data-writer sidecar whose configuration (write rate, retention logic, STORAGE EXTERNAL setting) and the PVC size reference need modification. The PVC itself (data-service-data) is a symptom reporter. An automated workflow can expand the PVC or modify the Deployment to add retention/reduce write volume.
dueDiligence.proportionality: Single Deployment (1 replica) is the sole contributor. The data-writer sidecar is the specific component requiring configuration change. Remediation options include: (1) expand PVC storage, (2) add data retention to data-writer, (3) remove or rate-limit data-writer. All are targeted to this Deployment.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: PVC spec and status, Deployment spec, Pod spec and logs (both containers), Prometheus volume stats (used, available, capacity, predict_linear), growth rate over time. Did not investigate StorageClass capacity limits on the node (lvms-vg1) — relevant for PVC expansion feasibility but not for root cause identification.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced from alert → Prometheus disk growth metrics → pod/container behavior → STORAGE EXTERNAL + no retention → undersized PVC. The chain is complete. The data-writer's design is the deepest reachable cause without access to application source code decisions.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All key claims are backed by tool output: PVC capacity (kubectl_describe), used/available bytes (Prometheus kubelet_volume_stats_*), growth rate (range query showing 84→113 MiB in 5min), data-writer behavior (pod describe + kubectl_logs showing 8 active batches), predict_linear confirming sub-1-hour exhaustion. No inaccessible data sources encountered.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for demo-archive/Deployment/data-service (confirmed via get_namespaced_resource_context — tier1 and tier2 history both empty).
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered: (1) PostgreSQL WAL/log bloat independent of data-writer — ruled out: growth rate matches 500-row batch cadence exactly, not WAL patterns. (2) External application writing to the DB — ruled out: only data-writer container is writing, confirmed by pod spec (no other clients). (3) Node-level disk pressure affecting the volume — ruled out: Prometheus metrics show PVC-specific capacity, not node-level disk.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: Starting at 1.0. Reduced by: -0.03 for not verifying StorageClass (lvms-vg1) expansion capability on the node (could affect remediation approach). -0.02 for range query returning only 2 data points (limited time window for growth rate calculation, though the rate aligns with the write pattern). Final confidence: 0.95.

contributingFactors: data-writer sidecar uses STORAGE EXTERNAL on the payload column, disabling PostgreSQL TOAST compression and maximizing disk footprint per row
Each batch inserts 500 rows with large string_agg(md5(random())) payloads (128 repetitions × 500 series), resulting in very large per-row data
No data retention policy, table truncation, or cleanup mechanism exists — data accumulates indefinitely
PVC provisioned at only 512Mi (filesystem-available ~448 MiB) which is undersized for a write-heavy workload of this intensity
predict_linear(available_bytes[30m], 3600) returns negative value (-332 MiB), confirming exhaustion within 1 hour
